scenario_config.name: national_medium
_dataset_notes: 10 teachers, 4 classrooms each, 25 students = 1,000 students total.
Each teacher has 100 students — representative of a teacher with 4 typical class sections.
8 weeks history: 6 cold (compacted to rollup tables), 2 hot (raw rows in Run/TurnEvent).
Hot data: 1,000 students x 2 hot weeks x 3 runs x ~20 turns ≈ 120,000 hot turn events (T=20 per docs/research/ingest-capacity-model.md).
Rollup tables hold 6 weeks of compacted history per student.
Analytics queries test at realistic per-teacher scale and with meaningful cold history depth.
Dataset prep will take several minutes due to bulk inserts and compaction of ~360,000 cold turns.
At the corrected steady-state ingest rate this scenario should be in reach of a tuned 2 vCPU box; the bell-burst regime is a separate test.
_traffic_notes: Derived from the corrected national load model in docs/research/ingest-capacity-model.md (medium adoption: 50% of 60,000 grade 4-6 pupils).
Steady-state CCU peak at medium adoption: ~4,243 students mid-run nationally.
Ingest RPS (steady): CCU / R = 4,243 / 400 ≈ 11 ingest/s (R = T × w = 20 × 20s = 400s mean run length).
Per-student rate λ = 1/R ≈ 0.0025 ingest/s — 1 POST /panel/api/runs/ingest/ per completed Run (no heartbeats).
The lesson-bell burst (~29 RPS for 60s) is a separate regime modeled by the lesson_bell scenario.
Teacher traffic: ~80 concurrent teachers at medium scale; dashboard ~2 RPS, analytics/turn_insights ~1 RPS each.
VUs are sized generously so drops come from server response time, not VU starvation.
At ingest p95 latency of ~200ms under healthy load: 11 × 0.2 = ~2.2 steady-state VUs; pre=80 gives wide headroom.
Expected outcome on the optimized stack: load_health green at this steady rate.
